briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome. We will now begin the climate briefing for June 13, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Escalating Climate Crisis Warnings and Extreme Weather Intensification Domestically and Internationally
sections.content: Domestically, extreme floods, once considered '100-year floods,' are now occurring with rapidly increasing frequency, happening 'every 8 years,' and thorough preparations are required for this summer [1]. Internationally, concerns are being raised that climate change is causing water pollution in the Iowa region [14]. Artificial intelligence (AI) analysis reveals stark disparities in climate change response plans among nations, underscoring the urgency of global cooperation [17]. These warnings clearly demonstrate that climate change is no longer a future problem but an immediate reality.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Carbon Neutrality Practices and Educational Outreach in Domestic Society
sections.content: Various local governments and institutions domestically are actively pursuing carbon neutrality practices. Yongin City Library is leading the way by establishing 'Green Libraries' to promote carbon neutrality [4, 8], and Gwangmyeong City strengthened its commitment to carbon neutrality with approximately 500 citizens through its 'Environmental Education Week' [5]. In Seoul, 542 schools are voluntarily participating in carbon neutrality efforts and expanding ecological transition education [7]. Cheongju City is encouraging 8,000 households to participate in carbon neutrality by providing incentives such as cash and local currency to those who conserve electricity and water [9]. This demonstrates the growing awareness of carbon neutrality centered around local communities and educational settings [3, 6].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Expansion and Grid Innovation
sections.content: Efforts to innovate power infrastructure for the transition to renewable energy are actively underway both domestically and internationally. Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) is pursuing grid innovation to align with renewable energy expansion, striving to overcome long-standing challenges [10]. Challenges related to innovation and standardization are also being raised in the field of renewable energy storage [11]. Furthermore, KEPCO has achieved success in overseas markets by securing project financing for a $500 million renewable energy business in Guam [12], and domestic company Unittest is expanding private sector contributions by winning a 60.1 billion KRW solar power generation project [13]. These efforts are essential processes for the stable supply and expansion of renewable energy.

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Technology Innovation and Investment Expansion
sections.content: Technological innovation and investment for climate change response are continuously being made. Notably, domestically, protein beads made from food waste have shown an innovative achievement by capturing 50% more carbon than existing Direct Air Capture (DAC) technology [2]. This presents the potential to significantly increase carbon capture efficiency. Internationally, Grey County has announced plans to allocate more budget to climate change initiatives [19], emphasizing the importance of financial support for technology development and application. These technological advancements and investments will be crucial drivers in overcoming the climate crisis.

sections.title: 🌍 Complex Challenges Surrounding International Climate Response
sections.content: The international community faces various challenges in responding to climate change. In some regions, movements to curb climate action have been observed, such as in Louisiana, where a bill limiting climate change-related lawsuits was signed [15]. Furthermore, discussions on identifying and responding to climate change fake news have emerged, making the accuracy of information a critical challenge [16]. Meanwhile, the impact of climate change on industries across the board is growing, with the perfume industry, for example, facing difficulties due to various factors including issues in the Strait of Hormuz and climate change [18]. In particular, with opinions suggesting that climate crisis denial is resurfacing, and a call for Australia to seriously address this issue while leading climate talks [20], a unified response from the international community is becoming even more urgent.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted that efforts towards carbon neutrality, renewable energy expansion, and technological innovation are being pursued concurrently, even amidst escalating climate crisis warnings both domestically and internationally. We must not cease our steps towards a sustainable future while addressing the complex challenges facing the international community. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Denial is back in vogue. As Australia leads climate talks, it’s beyond time we took the issue seriously
articles.description: It is unlikely that many voters are flocking to Pauline Hanson for her scientific insights – but that is where they are lining up, regardlessWant to get this in your inbox when it publishes? Sign up for the Clear Air Australia newsletter herePolitics is disconnecting from long-held assumptions at historic speed and no one knows where the great unhinging will take us. On the climate crisis, denial is back in vogue – depending on what the algorithm feeds you.One Nation’s surge in the polls suggest

summary.phenomenon: Climate crisis denial is re-emerging, and it is suggested that this issue must be addressed seriously, especially as Australia leads climate discussions. The rise in One Nation's poll ratings indicates this trend.
summary.cause: Politics is rapidly disconnecting from long-held assumptions, and climate denial appears to be gaining traction again, depending on the information fed by algorithms.
